request: Rank the 4 best Chevrolet Corvette generations overall across the full production run. Use 4 different generations, with one representative version from each generation.
title: TOP 4 CHEVROLET CORVETTE GENERATIONS
close_narration: Which Corvette generation is your favorite?
order_rationale: Ranked from fourth to first based on performance, innovation, and historical significance.

entries.name: C1 1957 Fuel-Injected
entries.label: THE ORIGINAL
entries.one_line_fact: In 1957, the Corvette introduced fuel injection, delivering 283 horsepower from a 283 cubic inch V8, achieving the magic one horsepower per cubic inch at a starting price of $3,333.
entries.narration: In 1957, the Corvette introduced fuel injection, delivering 283 horsepower from a 283 cubic inch V8, achieving the magic one horsepower per cubic inch at a starting price of $3,333.

entries.name: C4 ZR-1
entries.label: KING OF THE HILL
entries.one_line_fact: In 1990, the C4 ZR-1 stunned with a 375-horsepower LT5 V8, rocketing from 0 to 60 mph in just 4.5 seconds, all for $58,995.
entries.narration: In 1990, the C4 ZR-1 stunned with a 375-horsepower LT5 V8, rocketing from 0 to 60 mph in just 4.5 seconds, all for $58,995.

entries.name: C6 Z06
entries.label: TRACK MONSTER
entries.one_line_fact: In 2006, the C6 Z06 debuted with a 505-horsepower 7.0L LS7 V8, sprinting to 60 mph in 3.7 seconds, starting at $65,990.
entries.narration: In 2006, the C6 Z06 debuted with a 505-horsepower 7.0L LS7 V8, sprinting to 60 mph in 3.7 seconds, starting at $65,990.

entries.name: C8 Z06
entries.label: MID-ENGINE MARVEL
entries.one_line_fact: In 2023, the C8 Z06 redefined performance with a 670-horsepower 5.5L flat-plane crank V8, achieving 0 to 60 mph in 2.6 seconds, priced at $106,395.
entries.narration: In 2023, the C8 Z06 redefined performance with a 670-horsepower 5.5L flat-plane crank V8, achieving 0 to 60 mph in 2.6 seconds, priced at $106,395.
